content.text: I'll help you see your Docker stacks on Mittwald. Let me start by listing your projects and then showing you the available stacks.

input.prompt:
Use the available Mittwald MCP tools to:
1. List all projects using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list
2. For each project, list all containers/stacks using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_container_list with the project ID

Return a formatted summary showing:
- Project name and ID
- All Docker stacks/containers in each project with their details (name, status, image, etc.)

Make sure to call the tools in sequence - first list projects, then for each project list its containers.

content.text:
Use the available Mittwald MCP tools to:
1. List all projects using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list
2. For each project, list all containers/stacks using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_container_list with the project ID

Return a formatted summary showing:
- Project name and ID
- All Docker stacks/containers in each project with their details (name, status, image, etc.)

Make sure to call the tools in sequence - first list projects, then for each project list its containers.

input.description: List available MCP servers and tools

content.content:
Checking MCP server health...

mittwald: https://mittwald-mcp-fly2.fly.dev/mcp (HTTP) - ✗ Failed to connect

tool_use_result.stdout:
Checking MCP server health...

mittwald: https://mittwald-mcp-fly2.fly.dev/mcp (HTTP) - ✗ Failed to connect

input.command: cat ~/.config/claude/config/mcp.json 2>/dev/null | grep -A 10 mittwald || echo "MCP config not found or mittwald not configured"
input.description: Check Claude MCP configuration for mittwald

content.content: MCP config not found or mittwald not configured

tool_use_result.stdout: MCP config not found or mittwald not configured

input.command: claude mcp add --transport http mittwald https://mittwald-mcp-fly2.fly.dev/mcp
input.description: Add Mittwald MCP server to Claude Code

content.content:
Exit code 1
MCP server mittwald already exists in local config

- Project name and ID
- All Docker stacks/containers in each project with their details (name, status, image, etc.)

Make sure to call the tools in sequence - first list projects, then for each project list its containers.
